customCssDesc: Enter custom CSS styles here to modify the interface appearance. The system will automatically add !important to ensure highest priority. Changes will take effect immediately.

game3dPresetDesc: Generate a complete 3D platform collection game in one HTML file.

presetAutoSelectable: Allow collaborative tasks to select automatically
presetKnowledgeHint: Bind knowledge bases to each launch without changing workspace defaults.
deletePresetConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this preset? This action cannot be undone.
presetPreferenceOrderHint: Options are tried in selection order; the first available one wins.

presetTagEmpty: No tags yet. Create some to organize your presets.

deletePendingSkillNote: This will only remove the skill from the pending list. You can add it again using the "Add Skills" feature.

autoPreviewOfficeFilesDesc: Automatically open newly created Word, Excel, and PowerPoint files in the current project. Office temporary lock files are ignored.
computerUseDesc: Let the agent see and operate the desktop (screenshot, read window controls, click, type, scroll, reliably launch apps/URLs). When off, the agent is limited to tools like the terminal.
browserPersistentLoginDesc: Keep browser login state (cookies/storage) across sessions in an encrypted vault. On by default. Turn off if you need evaluate full-power mode (the two are mutually exclusive for security).
browserSilentDesc: Run the browser headless — no visible window. On by default (removes the pop-up window). Turn off to pop up a visible window, e.g. to watch the agent or log in the first time. Ignored on servers without a display (already headless).
browserSourceDesc: Which browser binary to drive. Either way runs an isolated managed instance with its own profile — never your real browser profile. Login state is kept in the persistent-login vault.
browserLoginDesc: Open a visible browser window to sign into your sites once. Later (silent) agent runs reuse that login — it persists in the shared profile. Close the window when you're done. Tip: don't run a browser task while logging in (a profile can't open twice).
browserLoginFailed: Couldn't open the login browser. If an agent browser task is running, stop it first, then try again.
browserLoginOpenedHint: A browser window opened — sign into your sites, then click "Done — close window".
browserSiteMemoryDesc: Let the agent remember each site's structure across sessions (stable element hints) to speed up repeat tasks. Off by default — this persists per-site interaction hints to disk and feeds them into the agent's context. Secrets are never stored.
browserTakeoverDesc: When the agent (in auto-approve mode) hits an irreversible browser action — submit / pay / delete / send — or a cross-origin data upload, hold it for your approval instead of blocking it outright. On by default. The prompt appears in the usual permission dialog; secret values are never shown.
browserUnrestrictedApprovalDesc: Dangerous: let Browser Use run irreversible browser actions and gated cross-origin uploads without asking. This only affects Browser Use, not shell, files, or desktop control. Off by default.
browserUnrestrictedApprovalConfirmConten: The agent will be able to submit forms, send messages, delete data, make purchases, and upload page data across origins without a Browser Use permission prompt.
browserUseDesc: Let the agent drive a built-in browser (open pages, click, fill forms, scrape). On by default; Chrome is fetched lazily on first use.
browserUseRiskHint: Browser Use lets the agent read and operate web pages. Turn on login state, site memory, visual screenshots, or risky-action approval only when the task needs them.
browserFullPowerDesc: Allow the agent to run arbitrary JavaScript in the page context. Off by default; enable only for debugging or tightly scoped tasks that truly need it.
browserFullPowerDisabledByPersistentLogi: Full-power evaluate cannot be used while persistent login is on. Turn persistent login off before enabling arbitrary JavaScript execution.
browserVisualFallbackDesc: When the agent can't anchor an element in the page structure (a stale or detached reference), let it screenshot the page and ask the vision model to locate the target, then click that spot. Off by default — every fallback sends a screenshot to the vision model (extra token cost). Secret regions are blacked out before the screenshot is sent.
computerUseAccessibilityDesc: Needed to read the accessibility tree and synthesize mouse/keyboard input.
computerUseScreenRecordingDesc: Needed for screenshots and the Set-of-Marks overlay.
computerUseRestartHint: If a permission shows "Not in effect": grant it to {{app}} (not a terminal/editor) in System Settings, then COMPLETELY quit and reopen {{app}} — macOS does not hot-load these permissions for an already-running process. If the toggle is clearly on yet still not in effect, it is usually a stale code-signing identity: remove {{app}} from the list and re-add the grant.
startOnBootDesc: Launch Nomi automatically when you sign in to macOS or Windows.
startOnBootUnsupported: Available only in packaged macOS and Windows apps.
startOnBootUpdateFailed: Failed to update start on boot setting
sendKeyDesc: Choose which key sends a message. "Enter" sends on Enter (Shift+Enter for a new line). "Ctrl/⌘+Enter" makes Enter insert a new line and sends only with the modifier.
keepAwakeDesc: When on, Nomi keeps your Mac awake while the lid is open, so scheduled tasks run on time. The display can still turn off normally (saving power and your screen — tasks keep running). Closing the lid still sleeps it and pauses tasks — unless it's plugged in with an external display and an external keyboard/mouse (clamshell mode).
hardwareAccelerationDesc: Use the GPU to render the UI. Disable if the app crashes on launch or graphics flicker.
hardwareAccelerationAutoDisabled: Disabled automatically after repeated GPU crashes.
hardwareAccelerationRestartConfirm: Restart Nomi to apply the new hardware acceleration setting?
hardwareAccelerationUpdateFailed: Failed to update hardware acceleration setting
promptTimeoutDesc: Maximum wait time for a single model request before aborting it.
agentIdleTimeoutDesc: Kill idle agent processes after this duration to free memory
modelModalityTip: Declare what this model does — probing and calls pick the correct endpoint from this.
apiUrlTipsHint: Click a provider to fill in its example URL
fullUrlHint: Use this address directly, no path appended
deleteModelConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this model?
deleteAllModelConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete all models?
modelDescriptionPlaceholder: Describe what this model is good at for automatic collaboration selection
pleaseEnterBaseUrlAndApiKey: Please enter base url and api key
multiApiKeyTip: 💡 Use a comma (,) between multiple API Keys; the system will auto-rotate
multiApiKeyEditTip: Supports multiple API Keys separated by a comma (,); the system will auto-rotate
apiKeyPlaceholder: Enter API Key(s); use a comma (,) between multiple keys
multiKeyTip: Multiple keys separated by a comma (,)
removeInvalidApiKeysBeforeSave: {{count}} API Key(s) failed validation. Remove invalid keys before saving.
customModelSupportNote: Note: Only Nomi currently supports custom models.
addModelHint: Pick or type a model ID and it will be appended to this provider.
addModelNoExisting: No models have been added for this provider yet. They will appear here once added.
addModelTips: You can paste any model name directly. Keep it consistent with the provider console for best compatibility.
modelProtocolTip: Choose the API request format for this model. new-api gateway will route to the upstream using the selected protocol.

restartConfirm: Changes will restart the application. Continue?
fontSizeDescription: Adjust the UI zoom level so the entire interface matches your preferred size.
webui.description: Use Nomi as your "24/7 Remote Preset" - arrange tasks from any remote device, anytime, anywhere.
webui.channelsMovedTitle: Channel settings now live with each companion
webui.channelsMovedDesc: Telegram, Lark, DingTalk, WeChat and WeCom are configured per companion now. Open a companion's Settings → Remote connect to manage credentials, approve pairing requests and choose which companion answers each channel.
webui.desktopLifecycleUnavailable: Hosting the WebUI service from the desktop app is not available yet (in development). Account and password management below still works.
webui.steps: Step 1. Turn on WebUI; Step 2. Copy the access URL; Step 3. Open in remote browser
webui.enableDesc: Start the web service to allow browser access
webui.allowRemoteDesc: Use remote software/server for secure remote access
webui.qrLoginHint: Scan the QR code with your phone to log in automatically on mobile browser
webui.qrAddressPickerTitle: The QR code will use this access URL
webui.qrAddressPickerDesc: If your phone cannot open the QR code, choose the IP address on the same network as the phone.
webui.initialPasswordDesc: Password generated on first startup, please change it
webui.passwordMinLength: Password must be at least 8 characters
webui.passwordTooShort: Password must be at least 8 characters long
webui.passwordTooLong: Password must be less than 128 characters long
webui.passwordTooCommon: Password is too common, please choose a stronger one
webui.usernameMinLength: Username must be at least 3 characters
webui.usernameMaxLength: Username must be 32 characters or less
webui.usernameFormatError: Use letters, numbers, hyphens, or underscores only
webui.usernameEdgeError: Username cannot start or end with a hyphen or underscore
webui.resetPasswordTooltip: Forgot password? Click to set a new one (no current password required)
webui.resetPasswordHint: Forgot your password? Click "Reset" to generate a new random password
webui.passwordResetSuccess: Password reset. New password is shown above
webui.passwordResetAndCopied: Password reset and copied to clipboard
webui.passwordResetAndFilled: Password reset and filled in form, please save
webui.cacheHint: Tip: when opening the WebUI in a browser, the browser may cache an older page and show content that differs from the desktop app (e.g. a mismatched number of themes or languages). Press Ctrl+Shift+R to hard-refresh and load the latest version.
webui.passwordHint: Password is shown in console on first startup. Use "Change Password" to reset
webui.checkConsoleForPassword: Check application console for initial password
webui.browserNotSupported: WebUI settings not available in browser
webui.browserNotSupportedDesc: For security reasons, WebUI settings can only be managed in the desktop application. Please open the settings panel in NomiFun desktop app to configure.
webui.passwordNotSet: Not set yet (generated when WebUI is enabled)
webui.passwordOnlyFirstTime: Password is only shown on first startup and will be hidden afterwards
webui.resetPasswordWarning: Please keep the reset password safe!
webui.featureRemoteDesc: Enable WebUI so your phone or remote browser can access Nomi.
webui.featureChannelsDesc: Connect Telegram, Lark, DingTalk, and WeChat to interact with Nomi from IM apps.
webui.companionToken.title: Companion access token
webui.companionToken.desc: Mint a token for a companion so an external MCP / REST client (such as Claude Code) can connect and drive NomiFun AS that companion. Keep it secret — leaking it grants the holder full operational access.
webui.companionToken.noCompanions: No companions yet. Create a companion in the desktop app first, then mint an access token for it.
webui.companionToken.revokeConfirm: Revoking immediately cuts off any external client using this token. Revoke it?
webui.companionToken.shownOnceHint: The plaintext token is shown only this once — copy and store it now. It cannot be viewed again after you close this.
webui.companionToken.noModelHint: This companion has no resolvable model yet; model-dependent capabilities will fail until one is configured.
channels.botTokenDesc: Open Telegram, find @BotFather and send /newbot to get your Bot Token.
channels.enableBotDesc: Start the Telegram bot to receive messages
channels.tokenLocked: Please close the Channel and delete all authorized users before modifying the configuration
channels.step1: Open Telegram and search for your bot
channels.step2: Send any message or click /start to initiate pairing
channels.step3: A pairing request will appear below. Click "Approve" to authorize the user.
channels.step4: Once approved, you can start chatting with Gemini through Telegram!
channels.title: Channels
channels.telegramDesc: Chat with Nomi agent via Telegram
channels.slackDesc: Chat with Nomi agent via Slack
channels.discordDesc: Chat with Nomi agent via Discord
channels.qqbotDesc: Chat with Nomi agent via QQ Bot
qqbot.tokensDesc: Create a bot on the QQ Open Platform (q.qq.com) and copy its AppID + ClientSecret.
qqbot.credentialsRequired: Please enter the AppID and ClientSecret
qqbot.intentNote: In the QQ Open Platform console → bot management → permissions, apply for the "GROUP_AND_C2C" intent, otherwise the bot cannot receive group/private messages.
channels.nostrDesc: Chat with Nomi agent via Nostr encrypted DMs
nostr.tokensDesc: Provide a Nostr private key (nsec or hex) — it is the bot identity — and a comma-separated list of relay URLs. Encrypted (NIP-04) direct messages only.
nostr.credentialsRequired: Please enter a private key and at least one relay
channels.twitchDesc: Chat with Nomi agent via Twitch chat
twitch.tokensDesc: Generate an OAuth token with chat:read + chat:write scopes and enter the channel name the bot should join. Twitch chat is text-only.
twitch.credentialsRequired: Please enter an OAuth token and the channel to join
discord.botTokenDesc: Create an application at the Discord Developer Portal, add a Bot, and copy its token.
discord.tokenLocked: Disable this channel before modifying the configuration
discord.intentNote: In the Developer Portal → Bot → Privileged Gateway Intents, enable "Message Content Intent", otherwise the bot cannot read message text. Invite the bot to your server (or DM it) to start.
channels.matrixDesc: Chat with Nomi agent via Matrix
channels.mattermostDesc: Chat with Nomi agent via Mattermost
slack.tokensDesc: In Slack API → your app: enable Socket Mode (gives the xapp- app-level token with connections:write), and install the app to get the xoxb- bot token.
slack.credentialsRequired: Please enter both the bot token and the app-level token
matrix.tokensDesc: Create a bot user on your homeserver and obtain its access token. v1 supports unencrypted rooms only.
matrix.credentialsRequired: Please enter homeserver URL, user id and access token
mattermost.tokensDesc: In Mattermost System Console → Integrations → Bot Accounts, create a bot and copy its access token. Interactive buttons are not available (they need a public callback URL).
mattermost.credentialsRequired: Please enter the server URL and bot token
channels.larkDesc: Chat with Nomi agent via Lark
channels.dingtalkDesc: Chat with Nomi agent via DingTalk
channels.weixinDesc: Chat with Nomi agent via WeChat
channels.wecomDesc: Chat with Nomi agent via WeCom (Enterprise WeChat AI Bot)
channels.guide: Connect Telegram, Lark, DingTalk and more to control your always-on Nomi AI agent from any chat app.
channels.selectFirst: Select a channel and configure credentials.
channels.enableAfterConfig: Enable the channel and start chatting with your AI agent.
channels.mediaPathNotAllowed: Couldn't send {{name}} because the file is outside the allowed send directories
mcpDeleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this MCP server?
mcpImportPlaceholder: Paste the MCP JSON configuration from the service documentation below. If the configuration uses commands like npx or uvx, make sure they are installed and available on your machine.
mcpImportTip1: Use a valid JSON object with a top-level "mcpServers" field.
mcpImportTip2: Each server should define either "command" (stdio) or "url" (http/sse).
mcpImportTip3: Multiple servers can be imported at once.
mcpEditNameLocked: MCP name cannot be changed while editing. Keep the top-level server name as "{{name}}".
mcpJsonBareServerError: Wrap the MCP server config in a top-level "mcpServers" object or add a "name" in an array item.
mcpJsonUrlRequiredError: HTTP and SSE MCP servers require a non-empty "url".
mcpJsonStdioCommandRequiredError: Stdio MCP servers require a non-empty "command".
mcpErrorNodeCommandNotFound: Command {{command}} was not found. Install Node.js (which includes npm/npx), restart Nomi, or configure this MCP server to use an absolute command path.
mcpErrorBunCommandNotFound: Command {{command}} was not found. Install Bun (which includes bun/bunx), restart Nomi, or configure this MCP server to use an absolute command path.
mcpErrorUvCommandNotFound: Command {{command}} was not found. Install uv, restart Nomi, or configure this MCP server to use an absolute command path.
mcpErrorPythonCommandNotFound: Command {{command}} was not found. Install Python, restart Nomi, or configure this MCP server to use an absolute command path.
mcpErrorDenoCommandNotFound: Command {{command}} was not found. Install Deno, restart Nomi, or configure this MCP server to use an absolute command path.
mcpErrorCommandNotFound: Command {{command}} was not found. Install it or configure this MCP server to use an absolute command path.
mcpErrorCommandPermissionDenied: Nomi cannot execute {{command}} because permission was denied.
mcpErrorConnectionFailed: Nomi could not reach the MCP server.
mcpErrorRpc: The MCP server returned an RPC error while handling {{method}}.
mcpErrorProtocol: Nomi could not complete the MCP handshake.
mcpLastCheckedAvailable: Latest check in MCP management: Available
mcpLastCheckedFailed: Latest check in MCP management: Failed
mcpNeverTested: Not tested in MCP management yet
mcpCheckJsonHint: Please review the MCP JSON configuration and test again.
mcpCheckPurposeHint: Used to verify whether the MCP configuration is available. It does not represent the real runtime status.
mcpInlineConfigHint: Configuration may be incorrect. Review the MCP JSON and test again.
mcpInlineCommandHint: Missing {{command}} on this machine. Install it and test again.
mcpTestConnectionFailedWithHint: {{name}}: {{error}}. Please review the MCP JSON configuration and test again.
mcpImportDescription: Import MCP configurations detected from your CLI agents into Nomi.
mcpImportSkippedPluginManaged: Managed by a CLI plugin and can't be imported here
mcpImportSkippedNeedsAuth: Requires authentication in the CLI first
mcpImportSkippedUnavailable: Unavailable in the CLI right now
mcpSyncFailedNoAgents: No available agents detected, unable to sync MCP configuration
mcpDeletedWithCleanup: MCP server deleted and configuration cleaned up

cdp.title: CDP (Chrome DevTools Protocol)
cdp.enableDesc: Expose a local CDP endpoint for browser debugging and MCP integration.
cdp.mcpConfigHint: Paste this into your MCP client config to connect to local Chrome DevTools.
cdp.restartRequired: CDP startup setting changed. Restart is required to apply.
cdp.playwrightMcpConfigHint: Use Playwright MCP to connect via CDP endpoint, with accessibility snapshots and browser automation.

needHelpConfigGuide: Need help? Check out the detailed
needHelpTooltip: If you need help, please check
googleCloudProjectPlaceholder: Enter your Google Cloud Project ID
appDescription: One desktop. Your AI agents, actually coworking.
bugReportModulePlaceholder: Select the module related to the issue
bugReportDescriptionPlaceholder: Describe the issue in detail, including steps to reproduce
bugReportScreenshotDropzoneText: Click, drag, or paste screenshots here
bugReportAutoInfo: System info and logs from the last 3 days will be automatically attached to help us locate the issue faster
bugReportSuccess: Report submitted, thank you for your feedback
bugReportError: Submission failed, please try again later
bugReportModuleSkillsDescription: For issues with the Skills Market, built-in or external skills, plugin install and enable flows, load failures, or plugin execution.
bugReportModuleWebuiDescription: For issues with WebUI startup, remote access, QR login, account or password changes, or browser-side behavior.
bugReportModuleMcpDescription: For issues with MCP server config, OAuth login, tool sync, missing tools, or tool invocation failures.
bugReportModuleChannelDescription: For issues with Telegram, Slack, Lark and other channel plugins, including config, pairing, authorization, or message delivery.
bugReportModuleChatDescription: For issues while sending messages, receiving agent replies, handling context, using the command queue, or other in-chat behavior.
bugReportModuleScheduledTaskDescription: For issues with creating, editing, triggering, reminding, or executing scheduled tasks.
bugReportModuleLlmConfigDescription: For issues with model providers, API keys, login and authentication, rate limits, quotas, or model selection.
bugReportModulePresetDescription: For issues with preset cards, presets, switching defaults, or preset behavior settings.
bugReportModulePermissionDescription: For issues where local or remote agents are not detected, unavailable, unhealthy, or fail to connect or complete a handshake.
bugReportModuleSessionDescription: For issues with search, history, rename, pin, delete, or how conversation lists are displayed.
bugReportModuleWorkspaceDescription: For issues with projects, uploads, folder selection, Office files, preview panels, or diff views.
bugReportModuleCollaborationDescription: For issues with collaborative task planning, Agent delegation, progress tracking, or participant message flow.
bugReportModuleDisplaySettingsDescriptio: For issues with theme, scaling, UI styles, or window display.
bugReportModuleSystemSettingsDescription: For issues with system permissions, launch at startup, notifications, storage paths, or platform integration.
bugReportModuleOtherDescription: Use this when the issue spans multiple modules, or when you are not sure which category fits best.
contactDescription: Use the official channels below to report issues or get in touch.

agentManagement.localAgentsDescription: NomiFun's built-in agent ships with the app — no install needed. Other agents are detected only after their CLI is installed locally.
agentManagement.remoteAgentsDescription: Only remote OpenClaw connections are supported for now. Other agents are in development.
agentManagement.marketEmpty: No installable agents found. Check your network connection and make sure GitHub is reachable.
agentManagement.marketContributionHint: Want a new Agent listed here?
agentManagement.customEmpty: No custom agents configured. Click "Add Custom Agent" to create one.
agentManagement.notInstalledDesc: Supported agents that aren't installed yet. Prefer the official docs for manual install; only a few verified commands support NomiFun-assisted install.
agentManagement.installHintUnknown: (no built-in install command — please look up the official method online)
agentManagement.installPrompt:
Please help me install the **{{name}}** command-line agent on this machine.

Official install docs: {{website}}
Verified install command: {{hint}}

Work within these boundaries:
1. First detect my OS, shell, CPU architecture, PATH, and whether the command's required base tools are available.
2. Use only the official install command above; do not replace package names or URLs with third-party articles, stale repositories, or search-result guesses.
3. If this command does not fit the current OS, or if you need to install/upgrade prerequisites such as Node, Homebrew, uv, or Git first, stop, explain why, and wait for my confirmation.
4. When the conditions are satisfied, run the install, then verify with `{{binary}} --version` or the product's official equivalent.
5. If login, a subscription, or an API key is required, explain the next authentication step without entering credentials for me.

If you hit permission, PATH, or network errors, diagnose first and propose the smallest fix before continuing.
agentManagement.remoteSupportedDesc: Connect to a remote agent gateway, or let Nomi walk you through it.
agentManagement.remoteSetupPrompt:
Please help me connect a remote agent to NomiFun.

I want to use the **{{protocol}}** protocol. Please:
1. Explain what I need on the remote side (the gateway/agent service and how to start it).
2. Walk me through getting the connection URL and any auth token/password.
3. Tell me exactly what to fill into NomiFun's "Add Remote Agent" form.

Keep it concise and step-by-step.

modelHub.title: Model Management
modelHub.subtitle: Choose free, local, or provider models by how you want to use them.
modelHub.globalConfigTip: Global default model settings for IDMM, the bypass model, memory summarization, and companion chat.

provider.title: Provider Models
provider.subtitle: Connect cloud model providers with your own API key.
provider.noticeTitle: Conversations are sent to the model provider
provider.note: Provider models run in the cloud, so avoid sending sensitive information. Custom provider models are currently available to Nomi only.

free.title: Free Models
free.providerId: No API key required. Enabled models appear in model selectors.
free.privacyTitle: Free models use third-party online services
free.automaticRefreshHint: NomiFun periodically updates the free-model list in the background. On failure, it keeps the current models and retries with backoff.
free.manualRefreshHint: The free-model list currently updates only when requested manually.
free.refreshHint: Fetch the latest free models from the trusted public source
free.refreshDegraded: Model-list update failed; the current models remain active
free.privacyNotice: Conversations are sent to the corresponding service and may be affected by rate limits, temporary outages, or policy changes. Do not send sensitive information.
free.loadFailed: Unable to load the free-model service
free.loadFailedHint: The managed model service may still be starting. Try again shortly or check the backend logs.
free.unverifiedTitle: Live availability is not verified yet
free.unverifiedHint: Built-in models are ready for selection, but NomiFun has not yet completed a successful online model update. Third-party inference may still be unavailable.
free.degradedHint: The online model list is temporarily unavailable, but cached or built-in free models remain usable.
free.emptyHint: Check your connection and refresh the model list. Built-in models are retained so first launch is never left without a model.
free.upstreamWarningHint: The model-list update encountered a problem. Current models remain available; try again later.

health.checkAllHint: Probe enabled models through the unified endpoint. This may take a moment.
health.checkingHint: Making a real model request. Please wait.
health.healthyHint: The model responded normally in about {{latency}}ms. Click to check again.
health.unhealthyHint: No valid response was received. Click to check again.
health.unknownHint: Click to check whether this model is currently available.
health.modelUnhealthy: Model is currently unavailable. Try again later.
health.modelUnknown: The model could not be conclusively checked.
health.batchWarning: Check complete: {{healthy}} / {{total}} available, {{unhealthy}} unavailable, {{unknown}} not checked
health.failed: Model availability check failed. Please try again later.

local.title: Local Models
local.providerId: NomiFun downloads and runs everything for you—no separate Ollama install.

capabilityCenter.subtitle: Private local AI, downloaded only when you choose
capabilityCenter.plannedHint: This local capability is planned and is not available in the current version.

local.privacyTitle: Your conversations stay on this device
local.privacyNotice: Prompts and responses are processed locally. An internet connection is needed only for the initial model and runtime download.
local.scopeNotice: Best for everyday chat and lightweight tasks. Use a provider model for complex reasoning, long-running work, or highly reliable tool use.
local.refreshHint: Check downloaded models and local runtime status again
local.loadFailedHint: The local service may still be starting, or this device may not be supported. Try again shortly.
local.emptyHint: The built-in catalog is empty. Update NomiFun and try again.
local.singleModelHint: Start with the recommended option. Only one model is active at a time; switching stops the current model automatically.
local.installSuccess: Download started. The model will be enabled and added to model selectors when ready.
local.cancelSuccess: Download paused; the partial file was retained
local.deleteConfirmContent: This deletes the local files for {{model}}. You can download it again later.

progress.verifyingHint: Verifying file integrity. Keep NomiFun open.

error.network: Network connection failed; retry from the saved checkpoint
error.checksumMismatch: File verification failed; download it again
error.unsupportedPlatform: This operating system or processor is not supported yet
error.runtimeUnavailable: The local inference runtime is unavailable
error.busy: Another local-model operation is in progress
error.notFound: The model or local file was not found

image.title: Local Image Generation
image.subtitle: Download Z-Image Turbo in NomiFun and create images entirely on this device.
image.refreshHint: Check image-model files, integrity, and runtime status again
image.onDemandNotice: The roughly 6 GB bundle downloads in real time only after you click Download & install. Nothing is preinstalled or downloaded silently. Pausing keeps every completed file and checkpoint.
image.loadFailed: Unable to read local image-model status
image.loadFailedHint: The local service may still be starting. Refresh again shortly.
image.empty: No installable image models are available
image.emptyHint: Update NomiFun to obtain the curated image-model catalog.
image.catalogDescription: A consumer-sized Z-Image Turbo bundle for fast local text-to-image creation. It uses a Q3_K diffusion model and Qwen3 4B text encoder.
image.actionFailed: Image-model action failed. Please try again.
image.installSuccess: Image-model download started; it will appear under Creation Models when installation finishes
image.pauseSuccess: Download paused; completed files and checkpoints were retained
image.resumeSuccess: Download resumed from the saved checkpoint
image.deleteConfirmContent: This deletes the runtime, three model components, and download checkpoints for {{model}}. You can download them again later.

readiness.artifactsReady: Runtime and all three model components are complete
readiness.unsupportedHint: This platform does not have a packaged image runtime. Current builds support Windows/Linux x64 and Apple Silicon Macs; no download will start on this device.
readiness.runtimeFailedHint: The installed runtime could not be used. Retry the installation; if the error remains, this device or graphics driver may not support the packaged runtime.
readiness.integrityPendingHint: Installed and already listed under Creation Models. Before the first generation in this app session, NomiFun will run a full SHA-256 integrity check and start inference only after it passes. This does not download the bundle again.
readiness.generatingHint: A local image is being generated. Heavy local-AI jobs run one at a time.
readiness.creationReadyHint: Integrity check passed. Select Z-Image Turbo under Creation Models to generate locally.

notice.title: Third-party component notice
notice.vae: The diffusion model and text encoder are Apache-2.0 and the runtime is MIT. The pinned VAE comes from Comfy-Org/z_image_turbo, whose repository card declares no license; preserve and review its upstream notices before redistribution.

error.network: Network connection failed; resume from the saved checkpoint
error.insufficientSpace: Not enough free disk space for the roughly 6 GB bundle and installation reserve
error.checksumMismatch: A component failed SHA-256 verification; retry the installation
error.unsupportedPlatform: This operating system or processor has no supported image runtime
error.runtimeUnavailable: The local image-generation runtime is unavailable
error.busy: Another local-AI job is currently using the runtime
error.notFound: The image model or local file was not found

asr.title: Local Speech Recognition
asr.subtitle: Download once to dictate into NomiFun input boxes offline. Paraformer is recommended for Chinese; Whisper remains available for general multilingual speech.
asr.loadFailed: Unable to read local speech-model status
asr.loadFailedHint: The local service may still be starting. Refresh again shortly.
asr.empty: No installable speech recognition models are available
asr.emptyHint: Update NomiFun to obtain the curated speech-model catalog.
asr.actionFailed: Speech-model action failed. Please try again.
asr.installSuccess: Download started. Voice-to-text will be enabled in input boxes after installation.
asr.cancelSuccess: Download paused; the partial file was retained
asr.activateSuccess: Local speech recognition enabled; input boxes now show the microphone button
asr.deleteConfirmContent: This deletes the local files for {{model}}. You can download them again later.

creation.title: Creation Capabilities
creation.note: The Creative Workshop detects image/video generation from the model name as a suggested default. If a platform's model isn't recognized, mark the whole platform with the capability below.
creation.markHint: Mark every enabled model under this platform as having this generation capability (overrides auto-detection).
creation.emptyHint: No image/video generation models were detected among your configured models. Add generation models to a platform, or mark one with the buttons above.
creation.emptyNoProviders: No model providers are configured. Add a provider and model under "Provider Models" first.

speech.title: Speech to Text
speech.subtitle: Choose a ready local ASR model or a configured cloud speech model.
speech.languagePlaceholder: Leave empty for automatic detection, for example zh or en
speech.enabled: Enable speech to text in input boxes
speech.noSources: No speech-to-text models are available. Enable local ASR or configure a cloud speech model first.

presetsHub.title: Presets
presetsHub.subtitle: Save Agents, models, Skills and knowledge scope as reusable one-click configurations.

mcpHub.title: MCP
mcpHub.subtitle: Register, import, and manage MCP server configurations.

openCapabilities.title: Remote & Open
openCapabilities.subtitle: Manage WebUI access separately from the NomiFun Remote MCP / REST capabilities exposed to external clients.
openCapabilities.flowWebuiDesc: Decide whether phones, tablets, and external browsers can open NomiFun on this computer.
openCapabilities.flowAddressDesc: The QR code binds to one real URL. Useless adapter addresses are filtered, and multi-NIC machines can still choose manually.
openCapabilities.flowMcpDesc: Pick a focused or full capability scope for external agents, then copy config or write it to a project.
openCapabilities.webuiDesc: Turn this on so a phone, tablet, or remote browser can open NomiFun. QR login and account credentials live here.
openCapabilities.addressStrategyDesc: NomiFun only shows URLs that are likely reachable from phones and LAN devices. Loopback, link-local, benchmarking, and reserved addresses do not enter QR candidates.
openCapabilities.ipFilterDesc: Hides 127.0.0.1, 0.0.0.0, 169.254.*, 198.18.* / 198.19.*, multicast, and documentation ranges. Keeps usable LAN/VPN candidates such as 192.168.*, 10.*, and 172.16-31.*.
openCapabilities.remoteDesc: Choose what platform capabilities external agents can see and call through NomiFun Remote MCP / REST. The generated access URL carries this selection.
openCapabilities.generatedConfigDesc: Use the MCP URL below in an external agent. Its domains parameter is the selected NomiFun platform capability scope; the Bearer companion access token is generated here too.
openCapabilities.domainAgentDesc: Run external agents, check agent health, and read available models and modes.
openCapabilities.domainConversationDesc: Create, read, send, and manage NomiFun conversations.
openCapabilities.domainBrowserDesc: Let external agents observe web pages, navigate, and perform browser actions.
openCapabilities.domainComputerDesc: Let external agents use desktop computer-control tools; no tools are exposed when the feature is disabled.
openCapabilities.domainKnowledgeDesc: Read, write, bind, and maintain NomiFun knowledge bases.
openCapabilities.domainFilesDesc: Read and write workspace files, list directories, and open files.
openCapabilities.domainTerminalDesc: Create, inspect, and control terminal sessions.
openCapabilities.domainRequirementDesc: Manage requirements, task workspaces, and requirement automation.
openCapabilities.domainAutoworkDesc: Enable, disable, and inspect requirement AutoWork state.
openCapabilities.domainIdmmDesc: Model dispatch, activity, and intelligent model allocation capabilities.
openCapabilities.domainCronDesc: Create, read, and maintain scheduled tasks.
openCapabilities.domainMcpDesc: Read and maintain connected MCP server configuration.
openCapabilities.domainExtensionDesc: Extension packages, sources, and extension state capabilities.
openCapabilities.domainSkillDesc: Read, import, and manage skill packs.
openCapabilities.domainHubDesc: Browse, install, and sync Hub capabilities.
openCapabilities.domainSystemDesc: System settings, theme, model providers, and client preferences.
openCapabilities.domainProviderDesc: Read model provider and available model information.
openCapabilities.domainCompanionDesc: Manage companions, bindings, remote access tokens, and companion profiles.
openCapabilities.domainChannelDesc: Manage IM channels, pairing, authorized users, and companion binding.
openCapabilities.domainConfirmationDesc: Read and resolve actions waiting for user confirmation.
openCapabilities.scopeAgentDesc: Advertises only the tools external agents usually need for task execution, keeping the tool list shorter and model selection more focused.
openCapabilities.scopeAgentDomains: agent, conversation, browser, computer, knowledge, files, memory
openCapabilities.scopeFullDesc: Advertises every tool allowed on the Remote surface, for clients that explicitly need platform management or deeper integrations.
openCapabilities.scopeFullDomains: All NomiFun tools allowed on the Remote surface
openCapabilities.projectRegisterDesc: Config stores only the launch command, never a port or credential. At startup, external CLIs receive workspace-scoped access through a local channel protected for the current OS user.
openCapabilities.mcpManageDesc: Open Capabilities decides what NomiFun Remote scope an external agent sees. Adding, importing, OAuth login, and connectivity checks for MCP servers still live in MCP management.
openCapabilities.mcpManageScopeDesc: External agents use the focused endpoint by default, reducing unrelated tools in model tool selection.
openCapabilities.mcpManageServerDesc: When you need third-party MCP servers, use MCP management for start/stop, authorization, and connectivity checks.

skillsMcp.title: Skills & MCP
skillsMcp.subtitle: Manage skill packs and MCP tool servers.

remoteAgent.title: Remote Agents
remoteAgent.empty: No remote agents configured. Click "Add" to connect to a remote agent service.
remoteAgent.name: Name
remoteAgent.maskedCredentialTestHint: The saved credential is masked. Re-enter the token or password before testing.
remoteAgent.allowInsecureHint: Skip TLS certificate verification (for self-signed certificates)
remoteAgent.deleteConfirmContent: Are you sure you want to delete "{{name}}"?
remoteAgent.pendingApprovalHint: Please approve this device on the OpenClaw Gateway
remoteAgent.pendingTimeout: Approval request expired (5 minutes). Please try again.
remoteAgent.legacyUnsupported: This legacy protocol is no longer supported. Create an OpenClaw configuration and migrate the conversation.
remoteAgent.hermesLocalHint: Hermes is supported locally through the standard `hermes acp` CLI. Its separate remote JSON-RPC gateway is not ACP-over-WebSocket and requires a dedicated adapter.

modelProviderHint: Used to group models in selectors and the Nomi — give aggregator platforms a distinct name.

factoryReset.title: Reset System Data (Factory Reset)
factoryReset.rowDesc: Wipe the database and all derived data, returning the app to a brand-new state. Use this to clear data left dirty by version iterations.
factoryReset.warning: This permanently erases all of your data and cannot be undone. No backup is kept.
factoryReset.clearsConversations: All conversations and their workspace files
factoryReset.clearsKnowledge: All knowledge bases and managed files
factoryReset.clearsSettings: All settings, model providers, channels, and preferences
factoryReset.restartNotice: The app will restart immediately and rebuild a fresh, empty database.
factoryReset.restartManually: Please restart the service manually to apply the reset.
factoryReset.failed: Factory reset failed. Please try again.
